channel.title: IO on Artem Yegorov
channel.description: Recent content in IO on Artem Yegorov

item.title: Modifying argument in a Ruby method
item.description: If you’ve been working with Ruby for a while, you probably know that arguments are passed into methods by reference to the object (more specifically, pass-by-sharing). Because of this, you cannot simply reassign a new value to an argument inside a method and expect it to change outside. Technically, you can assign it, but once the method finishes execution, the outer variable will still hold its original value. For example:
